commit.message:
Fix building placement: video intro overlay was blocking clicks

Root cause: The video intro div (z-index 9999) covered the entire screen
and continued intercepting clicks even after fading to opacity 0.

Fix:
- Set pointer-events: none immediately when video ends/skips
- Force-hide video intro div when startLevel is called
- Ensures canvas receives all click events for building placement

commit.message:
fix: wire coerce_numbers through encode numeric paths (#379)

Previously, the coerce_numbers field on EncodeOptions was accepted
by the CLI but never read by the codec. ZonedDecimal, PackedDecimal,
BinaryInt, and EditedNumeric encode paths only accepted Value::String
inputs, silently ignoring Value::Number.

Add coerce_to_str() helper that converts Value::Number to string when
coerce_numbers is enabled. Apply to all four numeric encode functions.

BinaryInt also gains a direct i64 fast-path for Value::Number inputs
(via as_i64()), avoiding the string round-trip.

Add 3 new integration tests:
- encode_coerce_numbers_comp3: COMP-3 with numeric input
- encode_coerce_numbers_binary: BINARY with numeric input
- encode_without_coerce_numbers_rejects_number: negative test

Closes #370

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: SIMD compilation mode - CPU portable vs native mode selection (#67)

This pull request introduces a new SIMD mode configuration system for
the FastPFOR crate, allowing users to select between portable, native,
and runtime SIMD instruction sets for the C++ backend. The changes
improve build flexibility, CI reliability, and documentation clarity,
while updating the crate version and standardizing feature usage in
development scripts.

### SIMD Mode Configuration and Build System

* Added support for selecting SIMD mode (`portable`, `native`, or
`runtime`) via Cargo features and the `FASTPFOR_SIMD_MODE` environment
variable; the build script now warns if multiple SIMD features are
enabled and passes the selected mode to CMake. (`build.rs`,
`Cargo.toml`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d0d98998092552a1d3259338c2c71e118a5b8343dd4703c0c7f552ada7f9cb42L16-R59)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-2e9d962a08321605940b5a657135052fbcef87b5e360662bb527c96d9a615542R28-R35)
* Updated GitHub Actions CI workflow to test all combinations of OS and
SIMD mode, and to cache builds separately for each SIMD mode.
(`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

### Documentation Improvements

* Expanded `README.md` with a clear explanation of SIMD modes, their use
cases, and configuration recommendations for end users.

### Development Scripts and Testing

* Standardized the use of the `_all_compatible` feature group in the
`justfile` for building, testing, linting, and coverage, and added
recipes to test all SIMD modes.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L4-R30)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L51-R65)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L95-R130)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L130-R145)
* Improved installation logic in the `justfile` for development tools,
with clearer output and better CI compatibility.

### Version Update

breaking and feature enhancements. (`Cargo.toml`)

New security heuristics from coverage gap analysis:
- H87: TRC curve anomaly detection (excessive points, invalid parametric
  function types >4, NaN/Inf parameters, degenerate all-zero curves)
- H88: Chromatic adaptation matrix validation (undersized chad, singular
  matrix det≈0, NaN/Inf, extreme values >100)
- H89: Profile sequence description validation (excessive entries >256,
  empty sequences, count overflow)
- H90: Preview tag channel consistency (Preview0/1/2 CLUT dimensions
  vs PCS channel count mismatch)
- H91: Colorant order validation (indices OOB vs ColorantTable count,
  duplicate indices)
- H92: Spectral viewing conditions validation (illuminant XYZ NaN/Inf,
  non-positive Y, surround NaN, CCT range)
- H93: Embedded profile flag consistency (reserved bits, conflicting
  embedded/independent flags, attributes reserved bits)
- H94: Matrix/TRC colorant consistency (RGB matrix column sum vs D50
  whitepoint, NaN in columns, negative Y, whitepoint deviation)

Coverage-informed: targets 101 previously-uninspected tag signatures
including TRC, chad, pseq, preview, colorant order, spectral viewing
conditions, and matrix/TRC profile validation.

Verified: 10/10 test profiles pass, findings detected on 12+ CVE PoCs

commit.message:
perf(kernels): vectorize softmax exp with AVX2+FMA polynomial (#3354)

## Summary
Replace scalar per-element exp() in both AVX2 softmax hot loops
(softmax.rs + simd_softmax.rs) with a fully vectorized Cephes-style
degree-5 minimax polynomial using FMA intrinsics. This eliminates the
store-to-buffer scalar-loop load-from-buffer anti-pattern that was
defeating SIMD throughput.

## What changed
**Both \softmax.rs\ and \simd_softmax.rs\:**
- **exp_avx2()**: New 8-wide vectorized exp(x) via 2^(xlog2e)
decomposition
  - Cody-Waite two-part ln2 range reduction (preserves precision)
  - Horner evaluation with \_mm256_fmadd_ps\ (5 FMA ops)
  - Bit-manipulation 2^n reconstruction (no libm dependency)
  - Max relative error < 2e-7 over [-88, 88]
- **softmax_avx2()** / **simd_softmax_avx2()**: Now call exp_avx2()
directly zero buffer bouncing

**softmax.rs only:**
- **softmax_avx2_inplace()**: New zero-allocation in-place variant
  - Eliminates data.to_vec() clone in \softmax_f32_inplace()\
- Reads and writes same buffer (each element read once then overwritten)

**All dispatch sites** now check both \vx2\ AND \ ma\ (all Haswell+ /
Kaby Lake CPUs have both)

## Receipt
- **what changed**: crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/softmax.rs (+123/-15),
simd_softmax.rs (+52/-11)
- **what ran locally**:
- \cargo build -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu\
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- softmax\ 399 passed
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --test avx2_scalar_parity_tests
--no-default-features --features cpu -- softmax\ 4 passed
- \cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- -D warnings\ clean
- **CI relied on**: CI Core (Build & Test, Clippy, BDD Grid Check)
- **parity evidence**: All 4 AVX2-vs-scalar parity tests pass (aligned,
unaligned, stability, pipeline)
- **perf receipt**: No perf claim (structural improvement; benchmark in
follow-up)
- **docs touched**: none
- **remains**: Could add a targeted microbenchmark; vectorized exp could
also benefit layer_norm and attention
- **disposition**: MERGE (A)

## Why this matters
Softmax is called once per attention head per layer per token. For a
32-layer model with 32 heads, that's 1,024 softmax calls per token. The
old code bounced through a scalar buffer for every 8 elements, negating
AVX2 gains. This keeps all computation in vector registers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(apple-silicon): NEON-accelerated attention dot product (#3355)

Implement NEON SIMD fast-path for Q·K^T dot product in the attention
kernel, replacing scalar fallback on ARM64 targets.

## What Changed
- Added `neon_dot`: vectorized dot product using
`vld1q_f32`/`vfmaq_f32`/`vaddvq_f32`
- Added `neon_qk`/`neon_qk_into`: Q·K^T matrix computation using
`neon_dot`
- Updated `dispatch_qk`/`dispatch_qk_into` to auto-select NEON on
aarch64
- Uses baseline ARMv8-A NEON (always available, no runtime detection
needed)

## Expected Impact
~2-4× speedup for attention Q·K^T computation on Apple M-series silicon.

## Verification
- 760 attention tests pass ✅
- `dispatch_qk_matches_scalar` parity test confirms NEON matches
reference ✅
- `cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --features cpu -- -D warnings` ✅

perf: optimize editor cache and nav panel per-frame work

Performance:
- EditorGalleyCache: store only layout sections instead of full
  LayoutJob. On partial cache hits (resize/zoom), rebuild LayoutJob
  from document text + cached sections — eliminates 1x document-size
  clone on every partial hit. Steady-state memory reduced by 1x doc size.

  or max_depth changes (was O(n) per frame).
- Nav panel: replace per-frame expanded_hash() O(n) scan with dirty
  generation counter — O(1) per frame.

the shared IHttpClient abstraction used by all other services. This gives
Foundry API calls consistent retry (axios-retry), error handling, and
request patterns.

- Add version picker dropdown to FoundryAgentDetails with auto-select
  of latest version on first load and stored version restoration
- Add listFoundryAgentVersions API with data-plane + portal BFF
  fallback and resilient response parsing (extractVersionsData)
- Add foundryAgentVersionNumber internal parameter to agent loop manifest
- Add useFoundryAgentVersions React Query hook in both designer packages

- Add post-save version refresh: flushPendingFoundryUpdates tracks

* fix(designer): sync version number and instructions to workflow params on initial load

On first agent load, the version number and system instructions were
not written to the workflow parameters, causing validation errors
(empty system message content). The initial-load effect now writes
both foundryAgentVersionNumber and syncs instructions into the
messages parameter alongside user instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): prevent infinite update loop and uncontrolled dropdown warning

- Use useRef flag for one-shot initial version sync instead of state
  guard that could race with batched Redux dispatches
- Add value and selectedOptions props to disabled version dropdown
  to prevent uncontrolled-to-controlled transition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): fix consumeVersionRefresh race condition and simplify Foundry code

- Add needsVersionRefresh() to check flag without consuming it
- Only consume flag after confirming new version differs from stored
- Extract shared helpers: useFoundryConnectionResourceId, getFoundryServiceContext
- Simplify foundryAgentService query params and extractVersionsData
- Update tests for needsVersionRefresh behavior in both designer packages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): remove unnecessary Foundry API call on version change, fix ReDoS and portal URL fallback

- Version selection no longer queues a Foundry agent update API call
- Portal URL omits ?version= when no version is selected (defaults to latest)
- Fix polynomial regex in buildProjectEndpointFromResourceId (greedy [^/]+)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): persist Foundry version change to workflow parameters

The version picker dropdown was not updating the serialized workflow
when changing versions. Two issues were fixed:

1. Type mismatch in handleVersionSelect: strict equality failed when
   the API returned version as a number but the dropdown provided a
   string. Added String() coercion for the comparison.

2. Stale preservedValue blocking serialization: dispatchParamUpdate
   updated the value segments but left preservedValue intact. The
   serializer checks preservedValue first, so it returned the old
   version number. Now clears preservedValue on every programmatic
   parameter update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): prevent generic agent UI flash for Foundry nodes

When clicking a Foundry agent node, the agentModelType parameter is
populated asynchronously via connection loading. Until it resolves,
isAgentServiceConnection is false and the generic agent UI briefly
renders before switching to the Foundry-specific view.

Added isFoundryAgentPending check: if the node already has a
foundryAgentId value, show the filtered Foundry-aware UI immediately
instead of the generic fallback, eliminating the visual flash.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(designer): add tests for Foundry agent fixes

- Add helpers.spec.ts: tests for isAgentConnectorAndAgentServiceModel
  covering Foundry params, missing model type, non-agent connector
- Add dispatchParamUpdate.spec.ts: tests for preservedValue clearing
  and String coercion in parameter updates
- Enhance foundryAgentDetails.spec.tsx: add handleVersionSelect
  logic tests for string/numeric version type coercion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): prevent Foundry pending state when switching to non-Foundry connection

isFoundryAgentPending now checks that agentModelType is truly empty
(not yet loaded) before returning true. Previously, switching from a
Foundry connection to a non-Foundry connection left foundryAgentId
with a stale value, causing the Foundry-filtered UI to persist and
hide normal agent parameters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(designer): consume version refresh flag unconditionally

Move consumeVersionRefresh() before the version comparison check
so the flag is always cleared after versions refetch. Previously,
if a Foundry save didn't produce a new version number, the flag
leaked and the effect would re-fire on every render.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logic-apps-shared): replace polynomial regex with iterative slash trimming

Replace /\/+$/ regex in normalizeEndpoint with a while-loop using
endsWith/slice to avoid ReDoS on strings with many trailing slashes.
Flagged by GitHub Advanced Security code scanning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(logic-apps-shared): remove portal fallback for foundry versions

Simplify listFoundryAgentVersions by removing the fallback to the legacy Portal BFF endpoint. The service now relies exclusively on the standardized Data Plane API.

- Remove projectResourceId parameter from listFoundryAgentVersions signature

fix: respect JsonNumberMode::Native in lib_api decode path (#378)

for numeric COBOL field types (ZonedDecimal, BinaryInt, PackedDecimal,
EditedNumeric), ignoring the JsonNumberMode option. Only the streaming
JSON builder in json.rs respected the mode.

- Lossless mode: returns Value::String (preserves leading zeros)
- Native mode: parses to i64/u64/f64, falls back to string
